perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information includes proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow. In general the receiving party must afford disclosed confidential information the same degree of protection as it would afford its own.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information includes proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow. In general the receiving party must afford disclosed confidential information the reasonable degree of protection.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the required degree of protection for confidential information. Originally, the receiving party had to afford the same degree of protection as it would to its own confidential information. Now, it only needs to provide a 'reasonable' degree of protection, which is subjective and less stringent. This contradicts clause 3a.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the standard of care required for confidential information from 'the same degree of protection as it would afford its own' to a 'reasonable degree of protection'. The original text held the receiving party to a higher standard, aligning the protection of confidential information with the receiving party's own proprietary data. The changed text weakens this requirement, creating a contradiction with clause 3a of the contradicted text, which requires 'all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained'. While clause 3a uses the word 'reasonable', the change from 'same degree' to 'reasonable degree' lowers the overall standard, creating a conflict. Therefore, it is an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Any specific documents or materials which are necessarily prov ided on loan for the above purposes will be specified in a schedule to this Agreement and the receiving party will return these and any other documents or materials subsequently provided to the disclosing party on request.
perturbation.changed_text: Any specific documents or materials which are necessarily prov ided on loan for the above purposes will be specified in a schedule to this Agreement and the receiving party is not obligated to return these or any other documents or materials subsequently provided to the disclosing party on request.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text contradicts the original agreement by stating the receiving party is not obligated to return borrowed documents. This contradicts the original intention, creating uncertainty about document ownership and use after the evaluation period. This contradicts with clause 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, or the copyright subsisting in them. PROVIDED that the obligations herein undertaken will not apply to: a) information which at the time of disclosure is in the public domain or which after disclosure becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the recipient, or b) information which the recipient party can show was in its possession at the time of disclosure or which is independently developed by the recipient and wa s not acquired directly or indirectly from the disclosing party, or c) information which is made public at any time by the disclosing party, or by others with the permission of the disclosing party, or d) information which is received by the receivi ng party from a third party without similar restriction and without breach of this Agreement. e) information which is required to be disclosed by legal process, law or regulatory authority.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the receiving party is not obligated to return documents, directly contradicting the original agreement. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that there is no transfer of ownership of any documents to the receiving party. If the receiving party is not obligated to return the documents, it implies a transfer of ownership or at least a perpetual right to possession, which creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties will mark or otherwise designate confident ial information to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties may mark or otherwise designate confident ial information to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will' to 'may' makes the marking of confidential information optional. This creates uncertainty, as without clear marking, it is difficult to enforce confidentiality, and contradicts clause 3 and 3f.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states parties 'will' mark confidential information, making it mandatory. The changed text uses 'may', making it optional. The contradicted text states 'the receiving party will receive all confidential in formation subject to the following conditions,' including clause f) which states 'will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information'. The change from 'will' to 'may' directly contradicts the mandatory requirement in the contradicted text to mark confidential information. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party
perturbation.changed_text: it may make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party
perturbation.explanation: The changed text contradicts the original agreement by stating the receiving party may make copies or abstracts without permission from the disclosing party. This undermines the confidentiality and ownership protection initially agreed upon. This in-text contradiction is in Clause 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the receiving party 'will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission'. The changed text says the receiving party 'may make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission'. This directly contradicts clause (e) of the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party'. This is a direct and clear inconsistency as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of one (1) year, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text reduces the period of confidentiality from ten years to one year. This creates an in-text contradiction because it significantly shortens the duration for which confidential information must be protected, impacting the long-term intentions of the original agreement, leading to uncertainty about its enforceability after one year. This In-Text contradiction is in Clause 5
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of one (1) year, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 10-year confidentiality period, which was then changed to a 1-year period in the modified text. The contradicted text is stating the confidentiality period is 1 year. Because the change directly alters the duration of confidentiality within the contract from 10 years to 1 year, there is a direct conflict and inconsistency as the agreement now reflects two different confidentiality durations, which can't co-exist.

perturbation.original_text: This clause shall not prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This clause shall prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the ability to seek interim relief, the changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the clause. This alters the parties' recourse in case of a breach, potentially hindering their ability to protect confidential information effectively. This In-Text contradiction is in Clause 6
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is to be construed and enforced in accordance with English Law and is subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English courts to which the parties hereto submit. This clause shall prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "This clause shall prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.", while the contradicted text states the same thing "This clause shall prevent a party from seeking interim relief in any court of competent jurisdiction." The original text stated the opposite of both texts, and the explanation states that the changed text contradicts the original intent of the clause. Since the explanation states there is an In-Text contradiction in Clause 6 and the changed text and contradicted text are identical, there is an In-Text contradiction.
